content: $$\alpha$$-particle is revolving in a circular path with radius $$r$$ with speed $$v$$, then find the value of magnetic dipole moment.
explanation:
Charge on $$\alpha$$-particle, $$q=2 e$$

Time period of $$\alpha$$-particle

$$T=\frac{2 \pi r}{v}$$

$$\therefore$$ Current associated by $$\alpha$$-particle,

$$I=\frac{q}{T}=\frac{2 e}{\frac{2 \pi r}{v}} \Rightarrow I=\frac{e v}{\pi r}$$ ..... (i)

Magnetic dipole moment,

$$\begin{aligned} & \mu=I \cdot A=\frac{e v}{\pi r} \cdot \pi r^2 \\ & \mu=e v r \end{aligned}$$

content:
Assertion : Paramagnetic substances get poorly attracted in magnetic field.

Reason : Because magnetic dipoles are aligned along external magnetic field weakly.

options.content: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true, but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true, but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.

explanation: When paramagnetic substances are kept in a external magnetic field, then it develops feeble magnetisation in the direction of external applied magnetic field i.e., magnetic dipoles are aligned along external magnetic field weakly. Hence, both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

content: The horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field at any place is $$0.36 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~Wb} / \mathrm{m}^2$$. If the angle of dip at that place is $$60^{\circ}$$, then the value of vertical component of the earth's magnetic field will be (in $$\mathrm{Wb} / \mathrm{m}^2$$ )
explanation:
Vertical component of earth’s magnetic field,

$$\begin{aligned} B_V & =B_H \tan \delta \\ & =0.36 \times 10^{-4} \times \tan 60^{\circ} \\ & =0.36 \times 10^{-4} \times \sqrt{3} \\ & =0.622 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~T} \\ & =0.622 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~Wb} / \mathrm{m}^2 \end{aligned}$$

content: The angle of dip, if dip needle oscillating in vertical plane makes 40 oscillations per min in a magnetic meridian and 30 oscillations per minute in vertical plane at right angle to the magnetic meridian is
explanation:
$$T=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{I}{M B}}$$

or $$T^{\prime}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{I}{m V^{\prime}}}$$

$$B=$$ Total earth's field intensity

$$V=$$ Vertical component of $$B$$

$$\begin{array}{rlrl} \frac{T^{\prime}}{T} & =\sqrt{\frac{B}{V}} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \frac{\left(T^{\prime}\right)^2}{T^2} =\frac{B}{V} \\ & \text { but } \quad V=B \sin \theta \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \frac{\left(T^{\prime}\right)^2}{T^2} =\frac{1}{\sin \theta} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \sin \theta =\frac{T^2}{\left(T^{\prime}\right)^2} \\ T & =\frac{60}{40}=\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{~s} \end{array}$$

Also, and $$T^{\prime}=\frac{60}{30}=2 \mathrm{~s}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \sin \theta=0.5625 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \theta=\sin ^{-1}(0.5625) \\ \end{aligned}$$

content:
Assertion : The magnetism of magnet is due to the spin motion of electrons.

Reason : Dipole moment of electron is smaller than that due to orbit motion around nucleus.

options.content: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
Both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
Assertion is true but reason is false
Both assertion and reason are false.

explanation: We know the magnetism of the magnet is achieved due to the spin motion of electrons. Also the spinning electrons possess magnetic dipole moment. This dipole moment is much greater than that due to orbital moment of electrons around the nucleus.
